surfaces.label: Page MAIN world to extension state
surfaces.notes: Public marker and unbounded nested objects cross into session state.

surfaces.label: WebSocket gzip and protobuf decoder
surfaces.notes: Compressed bytes are capped, decompressed and concurrent work are not.

surfaces.label: Captured values to extension-owned side panel
surfaces.notes: All reviewed remote values use textContent; no innerHTML, eval or remote script sink survived.

surfaces.label: Public profile fallback request
surfaces.notes: The HTTPS origin is fixed, the handle is encoded and credentials are omitted.

surfaces.label: DOM and webRequest media URL discovery
surfaces.riskArea: Origin allowlist bypass and signed URL exposure
surfaces.notes: Exact/dot-suffix CDN allowlisting and media classification apply before storage; debug export redacts query values.

surfaces.label: Side-panel commands to player and hook operations
surfaces.notes: No externally connectable runtime API exists; active TikTok-tab checks and fixed action allowlists constrain operations.

surfaces.label: Manifest and bundled executable code
surfaces.riskArea: Supply chain and remote code execution
surfaces.notes: All scripts are local; no eval, native messaging, cookie or all-sites permission is declared. ZIP hashes are handled by release QA.

openQuestions.question: Which per-message, per-tab, decompressed and in-flight byte budgets fit low-memory Chrome and Edge systems?
openQuestions.followUpPrompt: Benchmark only the future fixed build with bounded synthetic messages on a disposable local browser profile; record peak RSS and recovery behavior without using a live TikTok stream.
